title: Elon Musk Space Empire Forecast 2030 — Planet Labs, Rocket Lab, Intuitive Machines, BlackSky, and Gilat Satellite Are Riding the Structural Shift Toward Commercial Space Infrastructure, Lunar Operations, and Sovereign Intelligence
author: tickeron_community
selftext:
# Key Takeaways

* [PL ](https://tickeron.com/ticker/PL/) (Planet Labs) delivered record FY2026 annual revenue of $308M (+26% YoY), its first year of non-GAAP profitability, and guided FY2027 revenue to $415M–$440M (+39% at midpoint) with a $900M+ backlog — driven by defense and intelligence contracts growing over 50% year-on-year and multi-hundred-million dollar sovereign satellite services deals with Germany and Sweden.
* [RKLB ](https://tickeron.com/ticker/RKLB/) (Rocket Lab) posted record Q1 2026 revenue of $200.3M (+63.5% YoY) with a record $2.2B backlog, guided Q2 to $225M–$240M, and announced the largest contract in company history — a multi-launch Neutron deal with five dedicated flights plus three Electrons — with Neutron's first launch targeted for year-end 2026.
* [LUNR ](https://tickeron.com/ticker/LUNR/) (Intuitive Machines) reported record Q1 2026 revenue of $187M (approximately 3x Q1 2025), with $1.1B in backlog and full-year 2026 guidance of $900M–$1B, driven by the $6.24B Andromeda IDIQ, SDA Tranche 3 satellite manufacturing, and a growing CLPS lunar delivery portfolio targeting $20B in NASA opportunity across two phases.
* [BKSY ](https://tickeron.com/ticker/BKSY/) (BlackSky Technology) is the only profitable-path pure-play geospatial intelligence stock in the group, with 100% analyst bullish consensus, a current price of $37.48 against a high analyst target of $42, and a differentiated position in real-time AI-fused satellite imagery for defense and government customers.
* [GILT](https://tickeron.com/ticker/GILT/) (Gilat Satellite) is the only currently profitable name in this theme — P/E of 29.8x with positive EPS of $0.52 — providing satellite broadband connectivity infrastructure across emerging markets, rural connectivity programs, and defense applications, with analyst targets up to $20.
* [Tickeron's AI](https://tickeron.com/app/ai-robots/trending-robots/) identified this cluster as structurally coherent: all five names operate within the commercial space economy supply chain — imagery and analytics ([PL](https://tickeron.com/ticker/PL/), [BKSY](https://tickeron.com/ticker/BKSY/)), launch and platforms ([RKLB](https://tickeron.com/ticker/RKLB/)), lunar infrastructure ([LUNR](https://tickeron.com/ticker/LUNR/)), and satellite broadband ([GILT](https://tickeron.com/ticker/GILT/)) — representing the full value chain from launch to data delivery.
* Three names were added to the trending list within days of each other (May 31, 2026: [LUNR](https://tickeron.com/ticker/LUNR/), [BKSY](https://tickeron.com/ticker/BKSY/), [GILT](https://tickeron.com/ticker/GILT/)), signaling that Tickeron's FLMs detected a coordinated sector rotation into commercial space names as geopolitical defense spending narratives and NASA lunar program developments accelerated simultaneously.
* The overarching macro narrative is Elon Musk's SpaceX ecosystem — though none of these companies are SpaceX itself, all five benefit from or compete within the launch and space infrastructure market that SpaceX has legitimized and dramatically reduced the cost of accessing. 
* [RKLB ](https://tickeron.com/ticker/RKLB/) is the most direct competitive beneficiary, positioning Neutron as the only credible mid-size alternative to Falcon 9 ahead of regulatory and commercial diversification mandates.
